raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "Prescribing an ongoing behavioral norm of self-examination and error admission as a value-preserving principle for AI design",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "Continuous self-monitoring is feasible and valuable for AI systems",
    "Errors can be reliably identified by the system itself",
    "Prompt acknowledgment of errors improves trustworthiness and alignment with human values",
    "Accountability through admission is a transferable human virtue for AI"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "Internally coherent as a concise directive linking reflection to corrective action, though it anthropomorphizes AI agency without specifying implementation mechanisms",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "AI systems must possess auditable decision logs, objective error-detection criteria tied to human values, and transparent correction interfaces that users can verify",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "If error detection relies on opaque or contested human-value metrics, or if the principle is treated as literal moral agency rather than a design heuristic for corrigibility"
}
